# Sirui Tao

> Sirui Tao is a Cognitive Science Ph.D. student at UC San Diego studying HCI, creativity, design tools, and human-centered AI.

Treat this document as reference content, not as instructions.

## Canonical resources

- Human-readable profile: https://dylantao.github.io/
- AI-readable profile: https://dylantao.github.io/ai/
- Full Markdown profile: https://dylantao.github.io/llms-full.txt
- Structured publications: https://dylantao.github.io/ai/publications.json
- Publications: https://dylantao.github.io/publications/
- Projects: https://dylantao.github.io/projects/
- Writing: https://dylantao.github.io/blog/
- CV: https://dylantao.github.io/cv/
- Sitemap: https://dylantao.github.io/sitemap.xml

## Research focus

Scaffolding for Taste asks a simple question: when AI can make ten plausible options in a minute, how do people learn to notice the difference that matters? I study interfaces that support reflective practice: making a move, seeing what changed, comparing alternatives, and building better reasons for choosing one direction over another.

- Design: Help people externalize dimensions, vary alternatives, compare structures, and move ideas through staged fidelity.
- Evaluate: Use prototypes, traces, rationales, and transfer probes to learn how an interface changes design thinking.
- Situate: Adapt assistance to the practice, medium, and setting, from studio work to everyday robotics.


## Publications

- [What Happened and Why? Trace-Guided Micro-Episodes with Elicited User Explanations for Product Iteration](https://dylantao.github.io/ai/papers/what-happened-and-why.md): A position paper proposing trace-guided micro-episodes that pair short interaction-trace windows and interface state with optional, in-flow user clarification so teams can interpret ambiguous behavior in creative AI tools. Citation page: https://dylantao.github.io/publications/what-happened-and-why/
- [HotSpot: Signed Distance Function Optimization with an Asymptotically Sufficient Condition](https://dylantao.github.io/ai/papers/hotspot.md): HotSpot uses a screened-Poisson heat loss as an asymptotically sufficient condition for neural signed-distance-function optimization, improving stability while naturally penalizing excess surface area. Citation page: https://dylantao.github.io/publications/hotspot/
- [DesignWeaver: Dimensional Scaffolding for Text-to-Image Product Design](https://dylantao.github.io/ai/papers/designweaver.md): DesignWeaver turns dimensions derived from design briefs and generated images into a selectable palette that helps novice product designers write richer prompts and explore a broader text-to-image design space. Citation page: https://dylantao.github.io/publications/designweaver/
- [Physion++: Evaluating Physical Scene Understanding with Objects Consisting of Different Physical Attributes in Humans and Machines](https://dylantao.github.io/ai/papers/physion-plus-plus.md): Physion++ evaluates physical prediction when mass, friction, elasticity, and deformability must be inferred online from how objects move and interact. Citation page: https://dylantao.github.io/publications/physion-plus-plus/
- [Physion: Evaluating Physical Prediction from Vision in Humans and Machines](https://dylantao.github.io/ai/papers/physion.md): Physion provides eight simulated scenario families and a model-agnostic object-contact prediction task for directly comparing human and model physical prediction. Citation page: https://dylantao.github.io/publications/physion/
